tools.name: Counting Objects Lab
tools.description: Drag-to-count with ten frames, object grouping, and 1-to-1 correspondence. Large touch targets for young learners.
misconceptions: Students may not understand that the last number said tells the total
Students may count objects more than once or skip objects

diff.emergent: Counts up to 5 objects with visual support

tools.name: Number Recognition Engine
tools.description: Match numerals to quantities, order numbers, and compare sets using visual supports.
misconceptions: Students reverse numerals when writing
Students confuse quantity with the physical size of objects

diff.proficient: Matches numerals to quantity and compares

tools.name: Number Bonds
tools.description: Visual part-part-whole models for composing and decomposing numbers within 10. Build fluency with addition pairs.
misconceptions: Students think the whole must always be at the top
Students struggle to find multiple decompositions of the same number

diff.advanced: Find all pairs for a given number

tools.name: Teen Numbers
tools.description: Compose and decompose teen numbers (11–19) as ten ones and some more ones using ten-frames and base-ten visuals.
misconceptions: Students do not see that teen numbers contain a group of ten
Students confuse the 1 in 14 with one instead of one ten

diff.proficient: Identify tens and ones in teen numbers

tools.name: Shape Sorter
tools.description: Identify and describe 2D shapes from triangle to hexagon. Animated guided tour counts sides and corners with bright visual effects.
misconceptions: Students identify shapes only in standard orientation
Students confuse sides and corners

diff.proficient: Count sides and corners for all shapes

tools.name: Shape Builder
tools.description: Compose larger shapes from smaller shapes. Build pictures and designs while exploring spatial reasoning.
misconceptions: Students think shapes must be the same size to compose
Students struggle with rotation when fitting shapes together

diff.emergent: Combine 2 shapes to make a new shape
diff.advanced: Create original designs and describe compositions

tools.name: Number Compare
tools.description: Compare numbers and groups of objects using greater than, less than, and equal to. Visual dot groups and written numerals.
misconceptions: Students confuse "greater than" with "less than" symbols
Students compare by visual spread rather than actual count

diff.proficient: Compare numbers 1–10 with objects and numerals
diff.advanced: Compare written numerals 1–10, fill in missing symbols

tools.name: Add & Subtract
tools.description: Represent addition and subtraction with objects, solve word problems within 10, and build fluency within 5.
misconceptions: Students always count all from 1 instead of counting on
Students confuse addition and subtraction operations

diff.emergent: Add and subtract within 5 with objects
diff.proficient: Add and subtract within 10 with equations
diff.advanced: Word problems and missing addend within 10

tools.name: Measurement Compare
tools.description: Compare objects by length, weight, and capacity. Describe measurable attributes and determine which has more or less.
misconceptions: Students confuse length with area
Students think heavier objects must be larger

diff.proficient: Compare length and weight with visual models
diff.advanced: Compare length, weight, and capacity; describe multiple attributes

tools.name: Classify & Count
tools.description: Sort objects into categories, count each group, and compare category sizes. Build data sense through hands-on classification.
misconceptions: Students sort by personal preference instead of given attribute
Students lose count when categories have similar sizes

diff.emergent: 2 categories, 3–6 objects, sort by one attribute
diff.advanced: 3–4 categories, 8–12 objects, order categories by count

tools.name: Story Problem Theater
tools.description: Solve addition and subtraction word problems through animated story scenes. Drag counters to model ducks, apples, dogs, and more.
misconceptions: Students do not connect stories to math operations
Students count objects in the scene instead of modeling with counters

tools.name: 3D Shape Builder
tools.description: Build 3D shapes by assembling flat 2D faces. Explore cubes, cylinders, cones, and spheres. Count faces, edges, and vertices.
misconceptions: Students think 3D shapes are the same as 2D shapes
Students cannot identify the flat faces that compose a 3D shape

tools.name: Teen Number Factory
tools.description: Compose teen numbers (11–19) as ten ones and some more ones. Bundle cubes on a conveyor belt to build place value understanding.
misconceptions: Students do not understand that teen numbers contain a group of 10
Students think 13 means 1 and 3 separately rather than 10 and 3

diff.advanced: Decompose: given a teen number, identify tens and ones

tools.name: Pattern Machine
tools.description: Create, extend, and identify repeating patterns using shapes and colors. Recognize AB, ABC, and ABB pattern types.
misconceptions: Students only recognize color patterns, not shape patterns
Students cannot extend patterns beyond the visible sequence

diff.proficient: ABC and ABB patterns with 3 shapes

tools.name: Position & Direction Words
tools.description: Describe positions using above, below, beside, in front of, behind, and between. Drag objects in a room scene to learn spatial vocabulary.
misconceptions: Students confuse above/below with in front of/behind
Students do not understand that position is relative to a reference object

diff.proficient: Add beside and in front of
diff.advanced: All 6 position words including behind and between

tools.name: Number Path Explorer
tools.description: Explore a number path where each square holds one number. Tap, hop, and race along the path to build spatial understanding of number order and magnitude.
misconceptions: Students think numbers are just labels, not quantities with spatial position
Students cannot determine which of two numbers is more without counting from 1
Students struggle with before/after because they lack linear number sense

tools.name: Counting Sequences
tools.description: Count to 100 by ones and by tens using an interactive hundreds chart. Fill in missing numbers, count from any starting point, and race through counting sequences.
misconceptions: Students skip numbers in the counting sequence
Students cannot count forward from a given number other than 1
Students do not recognize the tens pattern in counting by 10s

diff.proficient: Count within 50 by ones and tens
diff.advanced: Count to 100 by ones and tens from any starting point

tools.name: Addition Number Line
tools.description: Animated jumps on a number line for addition and subtraction within 20. Bridges counting to operations.
misconceptions: Students start counting at the first number instead of from it
Students confuse the number of jumps with the landing number

diff.emergent: Addition within 10 with visual jumps shown
diff.advanced: Missing addend problems on the number line

tools.name: Fact Family Houses
tools.description: Visualize fact families with house diagrams. Explore the relationship between addition and subtraction facts.
misconceptions: Students do not see the inverse relationship between addition and subtraction
Students memorize facts without understanding the structure

diff.proficient: Complete fact family houses and see the relationships
diff.advanced: Generate fact families and explain the connections

tools.name: Equation Balance
tools.description: Seesaw balance model to understand the equals sign and solve simple equations. Make both sides balance.
misconceptions: Students think equals means "the answer goes here"
Students do not understand that equations must balance on both sides

diff.emergent: Make simple equations balance with visual weight feedback
diff.advanced: Create equations and find missing values

tools.name: Addition & Subtraction Fluency
tools.description: Mental math "strategies": make 10, doubles, count on. Build fluency within 10 and teen numbers.
misconceptions: Students rely solely on counting on without developing efficient strategies
Students do not recognize doubles or near-doubles

diff.emergent: Use make-10 strategy with visual supports
diff.proficient: Use doubles and count-on strategies fluently
diff.advanced: Choose and apply efficient strategies, explain reasoning

tools.name: Counting to 120
tools.description: 120 chart with pattern highlighting. Discover patterns in tens, count by 10s, identify sequences.
misconceptions: Students do not see the patterns in the 120 chart
Students struggle with counting across decade boundaries

diff.advanced: Explain patterns and extend counting sequences

tools.name: Place "Value": Tens & Ones
tools.description: Base-10 rods and unit cubes for composing and decomposing two-digit numbers. Drag to group and regroup.
misconceptions: Students see 14 as 1 and 4 rather than 1 ten and 4 ones
Students struggle with regrouping 10 ones as 1 ten

tools.name: Two-Digit Operations
tools.description: Base-ten blocks for adding and subtracting two-digit numbers. Model regrouping and exchange.
misconceptions: Students add each place value without regrouping
Students do not understand when and why to regroup

diff.advanced: Solve word problems with two-digit operations

tools.name: Clock Reader
tools.description: Analog clock for reading and understanding hours and half-hours. Interactive hand placement.
misconceptions: Students confuse the hour and minute hands
Students do not understand that the hour hand moves as minutes pass

diff.emergent: Identify the hour on a clock
diff.advanced: Identify patterns and connections between analog and digital time

tools.name: Length Comparisons
tools.description: Measure with units and compare lengths. Understand indirect and direct comparison.
misconceptions: Students do not align the starting point when measuring
Students use inconsistent units or mix units

diff.emergent: Compare lengths directly and order objects
diff.proficient: Measure with non-standard units and compare
diff.advanced: Connect unit length to number of iterations

tools.name: Data Graphs
tools.description: Create and interpret picture graphs, bar graphs, and tally charts. Organize and represent data.
misconceptions: Students do not understand one-to-one correspondence in graphs
Students miscount data when creating graphs

diff.emergent: Create simple picture graphs with visual support
diff.proficient: Create and read bar graphs and tally charts
diff.advanced: Answer questions about represented data and create graphs

tools.name: Shape Attributes
tools.description: Identify and describe shapes by defining and non-defining attributes. Sort and classify.
misconceptions: Students use non-defining attributes like color to classify
Students do not understand why shapes are the same despite size or orientation

diff.proficient: Describe and sort shapes by defining attributes

tools.name: Fraction Shapes
tools.description: Partition circles and rectangles into halves and fourths. Understand equal parts and naming fractions.
misconceptions: Students think parts must be congruent to be "equal"
Students do not understand that the size of the whole matters when naming fractions

diff.emergent: Partition shapes into two equal parts
diff.proficient: Partition into halves and fourths, name and compare
diff.advanced: Explain equal parts and describe partitions

tools.name: Counter Builder
tools.description: Addition & subtraction with physical counters, ten-frames, and counting strategies. Count All, Count On, and missing addend.
misconceptions: Students always count all instead of learning count-on strategy
Students do not organize counters to make counting efficient

diff.emergent: Add and subtract within 10 with physical counters
diff.proficient: Use count-on strategy and ten-frames within 20
diff.advanced: Missing addend problems using counting strategies

tools.name: Comparison Symbols
tools.description: Compare two-digit numbers using >, =, and < with base-ten block visuals. Drag symbols between numbers to build number sense.
misconceptions: Students confuse > and < symbols
Students compare only the ones digit and ignore the tens

diff.emergent: Compare numbers within 20 using visual blocks
diff.proficient: Compare numbers within 50 using place value
diff.advanced: Compare numbers within 99 and justify reasoning

tools.name: Word Problem Models
tools.description: Solve addition and subtraction word problems using bar models, number bonds, and counters. Visualize story problems with multiple representations.
misconceptions: Students do not know which operation a word problem requires
Students ignore the relationship between part and whole in problems

diff.emergent: Add To problems with visual bar models
diff.proficient: Take From problems with number bonds
diff.advanced: Compare problems using multiple model types

tools.name: Composite Shapes Builder
tools.description: Compose and decompose 2D shapes to build composite shapes. Snap triangles, squares, and rectangles together to discover how shapes combine.
misconceptions: Students think composite shapes are new shape types rather than combinations
Students cannot see that the same shape can be decomposed in multiple ways

tools.name: Place "Value": Hundreds
tools.description: Extend base-10 blocks to hundreds flats. Skip counting by 5s, 10s, 100s. Compare three-digit numbers.
misconceptions: Students may think 207 has no tens
Students compare numbers digit by digit from right to left instead of left to right

diff.advanced: Expanded form and skip counting patterns

tools.name: Word Problems
tools.description: Bar models and visual strategies for solving addition and subtraction word problems.
misconceptions: Students add all numbers in the problem without understanding the action
Students do not draw or use models to understand the problem structure

diff.emergent: Solve simple word problems with visual support
diff.proficient: Create and solve word problems using bar models
diff.advanced: Solve multi-step word problems and justify solutions

tools.name: Fluency Within 20
tools.description: Mental math fluency for addition and subtraction within 20. Quick recall and efficient strategies.
misconceptions: Students do not develop efficient strategies beyond counting on
Students confuse commutative property applications

diff.emergent: Use make-10 and doubles with support
diff.proficient: Quickly recall facts and use efficient strategies
diff.advanced: Apply strategies to solve missing addend and comparison problems

tools.name: Odd & Even
tools.description: Pair parade visualization for determining if numbers are odd or even. Connect to repeated addition.
misconceptions: Students think a number is odd/even based on the digit size
Students do not understand the pattern of odd and even

diff.emergent: Identify odd and even numbers with visual pairing
diff.proficient: Determine if a number is odd/even and explain
diff.advanced: Use odd/even properties to solve problems

tools.name: Array Builder
tools.description: Build arrays and connect to repeated addition. Explore rows, columns, and equal groups.
misconceptions: Students do not see that rows and columns can be counted differently
Students struggle to see rows and columns as groups

diff.emergent: Build arrays and count using groups
diff.proficient: Create arrays and write repeated addition sentences
diff.advanced: Create and interpret arrays, connect to multiplication

tools.name: Two-Digit Fluency
tools.description: Efficient strategies for two-digit addition and subtraction. Place value blocks and mental math.
misconceptions: Students add ones and tens separately but do not regroup
Students confuse the regrouping process

diff.proficient: Add and subtract with regrouping using multiple strategies
diff.advanced: Choose efficient strategies and explain reasoning

tools.name: Three-Digit Operations
tools.description: Base-10 blocks for three-digit addition and subtraction with regrouping. Model place value operations.
misconceptions: Students do not understand when to regroup across multiple places
Students lose track of regrouping in three-digit problems

diff.proficient: Add and subtract with regrouping in any place
diff.advanced: Solve multi-step problems with three-digit operations

tools.name: Measurement Lab
tools.description: Ruler tool for measuring length and understanding inches/centimeters. Estimation and unit selection.
misconceptions: Students do not understand the difference between different units
Students start measuring from 1 instead of 0

diff.emergent: Estimate and measure lengths with non-standard units
diff.proficient: Measure with standard units and compare lengths
diff.advanced: Select appropriate units and solve measurement problems

tools.name: Number Line Operations
tools.description: Interactive number line with hops for visualizing addition and subtraction. Jump size and direction.
misconceptions: Students do not count the jumps correctly
Students confuse the starting position with the first jump

diff.emergent: Make hops on number line with visual support
diff.proficient: Use number line to model and solve problems
diff.advanced: Create and explain solutions using number line strategies

tools.name: Time & Money
tools.description: Clock and coins combined. Tell time to the nearest 5 minutes and count money.
misconceptions: Students do not understand the relationship between coin values
Students struggle with intervals of 5 on a clock

diff.emergent: Tell time to the hour and identify coin values
diff.proficient: Tell time to nearest 5 minutes and count money
diff.advanced: Solve time and money problems with multiple steps

tools.name: Line Plots
tools.description: Create and read line plots, bar graphs, and picture graphs. Represent and interpret data.
misconceptions: Students do not align data points correctly
Students misread scales on bar graphs

diff.emergent: Create simple line plots with teacher support
diff.proficient: Create and read line plots and bar graphs
diff.advanced: Interpret data and answer comparison questions

tools.name: Shape Identifier
tools.description: Identify and describe shapes by their attributes. Explore angles, edges, and faces of 2D and 3D shapes.
misconceptions: Students think sides and vertices are different things
Students do not recognize shapes in different orientations

diff.emergent: Identify basic shapes and describe one attribute
diff.proficient: Describe shapes by angles, edges, and faces
diff.advanced: Compare and sort shapes by multiple attributes

tools.name: Partition Fractions
tools.description: Partition circles and rectangles into equal parts. Recognize halves, thirds, and fourths.
misconceptions: Students think all parts must be the same shape to be equal
Students do not understand that the whole matters in naming fractions

diff.proficient: Partition and name "fractions": halves, thirds, fourths
diff.advanced: Recognize and compare fractions of same whole

tools.name: Skip Counting
tools.description: Skip count by 5s, 10s, and 100s using an animated number line with hops and a toggleable hundred chart. Fill in missing numbers in sequences.
misconceptions: Students lose track of the count when numbers get larger
Students confuse skip counting with regular counting

tools.name: Addition Strategies
tools.description: Explore four addition strategies side-by-side: Make a Ten, Decompose, Number Line, and Compensation. Step-by-step visual walkthroughs with explanations.
misconceptions: Students rely on only one strategy and cannot flexibly choose
Students do not understand WHY a strategy works, only the steps

diff.advanced: Explain why each strategy works using place value

tools.name: Picture Graph Builder
tools.description: Build picture graphs and bar graphs from data sets with up to 4 categories. Answer compare, total, and difference questions from the graphs.
misconceptions: Students miscount picture icons in a graph
Students confuse the category label with the count
Students struggle with comparison questions (how many more)

tools.name: Equal Shares Partitioner
tools.description: Partition circles and rectangles into halves, thirds, and fourths. Learn that equal shares must be equal in size, and that the whole equals the sum of its parts.
misconceptions: Students make unequal partitions and call them equal
Students think halves must look identical (cannot be different shapes)
Students cannot connect partitioning to fraction language

diff.advanced: Halves, thirds, and fourths, both shapes, different orientations

tools.name: Multiplication Array Builder
tools.description: Build visual arrays to understand multiplication as equal groups. See rows x columns, commutative property, and fact families.
misconceptions: Students confuse rows and columns
Students cannot connect arrays to multiplication equations
Students do not see that 3x4 = 4x3

diff.emergent: Factors 1-5, build arrays up to 25 dots
diff.proficient: Factors 1-10, commutative property, multiple arrays for same product
diff.advanced: Factors 1-12, fact families connecting multiplication and division

tools.name: Division Fair Share
tools.description: Distribute objects equally among groups to understand division. Drag cookies onto plates, connect division to multiplication.
misconceptions: Students confuse partition and measurement division models
Students cannot connect division back to multiplication

diff.proficient: Dividends to 50, both models, multiplication connection
diff.advanced: Dividends to 100, fact families, conceptual remainders

tools.name: Fact Fluency ×÷
tools.description: Timed multiplication and division fact practice with mastery tracking. Interactive times table, flash cards, and speed rounds.
misconceptions: Students rely on skip-counting instead of recall
Students know multiplication but cannot quickly retrieve division facts

diff.advanced: x2-x12 all operations, including hard facts (7x8, 6x9)

tools.name: Two-Step Word Problems
tools.description: Multi-step word problems using +, -, x, ÷ with step-by-step visual breakdowns. Uses diverse characters and real-world contexts.
misconceptions: Students solve only the first step
Students cannot identify which operations to use
Students confuse the order of steps

tools.name: Patterns & Rules
tools.description: Identify arithmetic patterns in multiplication tables. Discover rules like multiples of 5 end in 0 or 5, and cross-row relationships.
misconceptions: Students memorize patterns without understanding why they work
Students cannot extend patterns to new situations

tools.name: Rounding Roller
tools.description: Round whole numbers to nearest 10 or 100 using a visual number line. See where numbers fall between benchmarks.
misconceptions: Students round to the wrong benchmark
Students do not understand why we round up at 5

tools.name: Multi-Digit Add & Sub
tools.description: Add and subtract within 1000 using place value strategies and standard algorithm with visual regrouping.
misconceptions: Students regroup incorrectly (carry/borrow errors)
Students subtract the smaller digit from larger regardless of position

tools.name: Fraction Bars Explorer
tools.description: Interactive fraction bars for understanding unit fractions, equivalence, and comparison. Color-coded by denominator.
misconceptions: Students think larger denominators mean larger fractions
Students do not see fractions as a single number

diff.proficient: Add sixths; compare fractions, find equivalents

tools.name: Equivalent Fractions
tools.description: Discover that fractions can look different but represent the same amount. Compare bars side by side to find equivalences.
misconceptions: Students think different-looking fractions cannot be equal
Students multiply/divide only numerator or only denominator

tools.name: Fractions on a Number Line
tools.description: Place fractions on a number line from 0 to 1. Drag markers, discover that fractions are numbers with positions.
misconceptions: Students do not see fractions as points on a number line
Students confuse the number of partitions with the fraction value

tools.name: Area & Perimeter Lab
tools.description: Build rectangles on a grid, count squares for area, count edges for perimeter. Discover same area, different perimeter.
misconceptions: Students confuse area and perimeter
Students think same perimeter means same area

diff.advanced: Given dimensions, real-world contexts, same area/different perimeter

tools.name: Elapsed Time
tools.description: Tell time to the nearest minute and solve elapsed time problems. Two clocks with a time number line between them.
misconceptions: Students count elapsed minutes incorrectly across the hour
Students confuse AM/PM
Students subtract times like regular numbers

tools.name: Scaled Graphs
tools.description: Draw and interpret scaled picture graphs and bar graphs where each unit represents more than 1.
misconceptions: Students forget to multiply by the scale
Students cannot choose an appropriate scale

tools.name: Shape Partitioner
tools.description: Partition rectangles and circles into equal parts and connect to fractions. See that equal parts must be equal SIZE.
misconceptions: Students partition into unequal pieces and still call them fractions
Students cannot partition circles into odd numbers of parts

diff.advanced: All partitions including eighths, multiple representations

tools.name: Quadrilateral Sorter
tools.description: Classify quadrilaterals by "attributes": sides, angles, parallel sides. Discover that a square IS a rectangle IS a rhombus.
misconceptions: Students think squares are not rectangles
Students classify by appearance rather than attributes
Students confuse parallel and perpendicular

tools.name: Unknown Factor Finder
tools.description: Find the unknown number in multiplication and division equations. See that division IS finding an unknown factor — the bridge from arithmetic to algebra.
misconceptions: Students cannot see division as the inverse of multiplication
Students do not connect ? × 4 = 20 to 20 ÷ 4 = ?
Students guess instead of using fact families

diff.emergent: Facts to 5×5, unknown always first position
diff.proficient: Facts to 10×10, unknown in any position
diff.advanced: Facts to 12×12, mixed multiplication and division forms

tools.name: Properties Explorer
tools.description: Explore commutative, associative, and distributive properties with arrays and area models. These are the rules that make algebra work.
misconceptions: Students think commutative property means numbers are the same
Students cannot apply distributive property to break apart harder facts
Students confuse associative and commutative properties

tools.name: Multiply by Tens
tools.description: Multiply one-digit numbers by multiples of 10 using place value blocks. See the "pattern": multiply the digits, then add the zero.
misconceptions: Students do not connect 3×4=12 to 3×40=120
Students forget the zero when multiplying by tens
Students cannot extend the pattern to ×100 or ×1000

tools.name: Liquid Volume & Mass
tools.description: Measure and estimate liquid volumes (liters) and masses (grams, kilograms) using virtual graduated cylinders and balance scales.
misconceptions: Students confuse mass and weight
Students cannot estimate reasonable masses for everyday objects
Students struggle reading graduated cylinder markings

diff.proficient: Larger values, word problems with one operation

tools.name: Line Plot Builder
tools.description: Measure objects with rulers marked in halves and fourths, then display the data on a line plot. Read and interpret fractional measurement data.
misconceptions: Students cannot read fractional markings on a ruler
Students confuse line plot position with value
Students cannot interpret stacked X marks as frequency

tools.name: Multiplicative Comparison
tools.description: Understand multiplication as comparison. "3 times as many" with bar diagrams, comparison word problems, and trading contexts.
misconceptions: Students confuse additive and multiplicative comparison
Students reverse the multiplier and the group size

tools.name: Multi-Step Problem Solver
tools.description: Solve multi-step word problems using all four operations. Interpret remainders in "context": round up, drop, or use as the answer.
misconceptions: Students ignore remainders
Students always round up regardless of context
Students skip steps in multi-step problems

tools.name: Factors & Multiples Explorer
tools.description: Find factor pairs using rainbow diagrams, identify prime vs composite numbers, and generate multiples. Beadwork tiling connection.
misconceptions: Students confuse factors and multiples
Students think 1 is prime
Students miss factor pairs

tools.name: Pattern Generator
tools.description: Generate and analyze number patterns using rules. Input/output tables scaffold toward function thinking. Key algebraic bridge tool.
misconceptions: Students see only one step ahead
Students confuse additive and multiplicative rules
Students struggle to articulate rules

tools.name: Place Value Relationships
tools.description: Explore the 10× relationship between place values. Read, write, and compare multi-digit numbers in standard, expanded, and word form.
misconceptions: Students confuse face value with place value
Students struggle with the 10× relationship between adjacent places

tools.name: Rounding Workshop
tools.description: Round multi-digit numbers to any place using a visual number line. Zoom in to see which benchmark a number is closer to.
misconceptions: Students always round up
Students round to wrong place value
Students struggle with rounding across zeros

diff.proficient: Round to any place, multi-step estimation

tools.name: Multi-Digit Add & Subtract
tools.description: Standard algorithm for addition and subtraction up to 1,000,000 with animated regrouping. Place value columns highlight step by step.
misconceptions: Students forget to regroup
Students subtract smaller from larger regardless of position
Students struggle with zeros

tools.name: Area Model Multiplication
tools.description: Three methods side by "side": area model, partial products, and standard algorithm. Multiply up to 4-digit × 1-digit and 2-digit × 2-digit.
misconceptions: Students forget partial products
Students misalign place values in standard algorithm
Students confuse area model sections

tools.name: Division Explorer
tools.description: Long division with visual place-value scaffold. Divide up to 4-digit by 1-digit. Animated bring-down steps and remainder interpretation.
misconceptions: Students skip place values in quotient
Students forget to bring down
Students confuse remainder with quotient

tools.name: Equivalent Fractions Lab
tools.description: Discover equivalent fractions with fraction bars, circles, and number lines. Multiply numerator and denominator by the same number.
misconceptions: Students think multiplying makes fractions bigger
Students only multiply numerator
Students confuse equivalent with equal parts

tools.name: Fraction Comparator
tools.description: Compare fractions using visual models and "strategies": same denominator, same numerator, benchmark to 1/2, and common denominators.
misconceptions: Students think larger denominator means larger fraction
Students compare numerators without considering denominators

tools.name: Fraction Add & Subtract
tools.description: Add and subtract fractions with like denominators using visual fraction bars. Mixed numbers with regrouping. Decompose fractions.
misconceptions: Students add denominators too
Students struggle with mixed number regrouping
Students cannot decompose fractions

tools.name: Fraction × Whole Number
tools.description: Multiply a fraction by a whole number using repeated addition, number line jumps, and equation form. Convert to mixed numbers.
misconceptions: Students multiply both numerator and denominator by the whole number
Students forget to simplify or convert to mixed number

tools.name: Decimal Fractions
tools.description: Connect fractions to decimals using tenths and hundredths grids. Convert between fraction and decimal notation. Compare decimals.
misconceptions: Students think 0.3 > 0.30 because 3 > 30 reversed
Students struggle connecting grid shading to decimal notation

diff.proficient: Mixed tenths/hundredths, compare decimals to fractions

tools.name: Measurement Converter
tools.description: Convert between measurement "units": length, mass, volume, time. Includes body-proportional measurement (fathom, cubit) per MN Indigenous mandate.
misconceptions: Students multiply when should divide
Students confuse metric and customary units
Students forget multi-step conversions

tools.name: Area & Perimeter Formulas
tools.description: Discover and apply A = l × w and P = 2l + 2w. Design "challenges": same area different perimeter. Fish rack and garden contexts.
misconceptions: Students confuse area and perimeter
Students forget to multiply both dimensions for perimeter
Students think same area means same perimeter

tools.name: Angle Explorer
tools.description: Create, measure, and classify angles. Virtual protractor. Additive angle problems. Dakota star knowledge connection for angles.
misconceptions: Students think longer rays mean bigger angles
Students read the wrong protractor scale
Students confuse acute and obtuse

tools.name: Lines, Rays & Angles
tools.description: Draw and identify points, lines, line segments, rays. Discover parallel and perpendicular relationships. Fish rack construction context.
misconceptions: Students confuse lines and line segments
Students think parallel lines must be same length
Students miss perpendicular relationships

tools.name: Shape Classifier
tools.description: Classify triangles by angles and sides. Master the quadrilateral "hierarchy": parallelogram → rectangle → square. Shapes in traditional architecture.
misconceptions: Students think a square is not a rectangle
Students classify only by appearance not properties
Students confuse isosceles and equilateral

tools.name: Line Symmetry
tools.description: Identify and draw lines of symmetry. Fold simulation. Create symmetric designs. Anishinaabe birch bark biting (mazinibaganjigan) connection.
misconceptions: Students think all shapes have symmetry
Students confuse line of symmetry with any line through center
Students miss diagonal lines of symmetry

diff.proficient: Create symmetric designs, rotational vs reflective

tools.name: Order of Operations
tools.description: Evaluate expressions using parentheses, brackets, and braces. Step-by-step PEMDAS evaluator highlights operations in order.
misconceptions: Students evaluate left to right ignoring operation hierarchy
Students forget to evaluate inside parentheses first

tools.name: Expression Writer
tools.description: Translate between verbal descriptions and numerical expressions. Interpret expressions without evaluating.
misconceptions: Students confuse "less than" with subtraction order
Students struggle with nested operations in words

tools.name: Numerical Patterns
tools.description: Generate two patterns from rules, compare corresponding terms, form ordered pairs and graph on coordinate plane.
misconceptions: Students apply rules additively instead of multiplicatively
Students confuse x and y coordinates when plotting

tools.name: Powers of 10 Explorer
tools.description: Explore place value relationships where each digit is 10× its neighbor. Multiply and divide by powers of 10 with exponent notation.
misconceptions: Students count zeros instead of understanding place shifts
Students move decimal the wrong direction when dividing

diff.proficient: Divide by powers of 10, explain patterns

tools.name: Decimal Place Value
tools.description: Read, write, and compare decimals to thousandths in standard, expanded, and word form. Round decimals to any place.
misconceptions: Students think longer decimals are larger (0.12 > 0.9)
Students round to wrong place value

tools.name: Decimal Compare & Round
tools.description: Compare pairs of decimals using >, <, = on a visual number line. Round decimals to any place with visual support.
misconceptions: Students compare decimal digits without aligning place values
Students think rounding always goes up

tools.name: Multi-Digit Multiplication
tools.description: Fluently multiply multi-digit whole numbers using the standard algorithm with area model visual support and partial products.
misconceptions: Students misalign partial products
Students forget to add a zero placeholder row

tools.name: Long Division
tools.description: Divide up to 4-digit dividends by 2-digit divisors using area models, arrays, and the standard algorithm with scaffolding.
misconceptions: Students subtract incorrectly during long division
Students forget to bring down the next digit

tools.name: Decimal Operations Lab
tools.description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths using hundredths grids, place value models, and strategies.
misconceptions: Students misalign decimal points when adding
Students ignore decimal placement when multiplying

tools.name: Fraction Add & Subtract
tools.description: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators using fraction bars, common denominator visualization, and mixed numbers.
misconceptions: Students add numerators AND denominators
Students find LCD but forget to adjust numerators

tools.name: Fractions as Division
tools.description: Interpret a/b as a ÷ b. Fair sharing "model": divide 3 cookies among 4 friends to discover each gets 3/4.
misconceptions: Students see fractions and division as unrelated concepts
Students confuse numerator/denominator with dividend/divisor order

diff.proficient: Word problems with mixed number results

tools.name: Fraction Multiplication
tools.description: Multiply fractions using area models and tape diagrams. Visualize fraction × whole and fraction × fraction products.
misconceptions: Students think multiplication always makes numbers bigger
Students add instead of multiply numerators

tools.name: Multiplication as Scaling
tools.description: Understand multiplication as scaling. Predict whether products grow, shrink, or stay the same based on the multiplier.
misconceptions: Students always expect multiplication to increase
Students think multiplying by 1/2 is dividing by 1/2

diff.emergent: Scale by whole numbers and unit fractions

tools.name: Fraction Division
tools.description: Divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions. Visual partitioning and grouping models.
misconceptions: Students invert the wrong fraction
Students think dividing by a fraction makes the answer smaller

tools.name: Fraction Word Problems
tools.description: Solve real-world fraction problems using bar models. Multi-step problems with all four fraction operations.
misconceptions: Students choose wrong operation for the context
Students forget to simplify or convert mixed numbers

tools.name: Unit Conversion
tools.description: Convert between metric and customary units within measurement systems. Double number lines and conversion tables.
misconceptions: Students multiply when they should divide (or vice versa)
Students confuse metric and customary units

tools.name: Line Plots with Fractions
tools.description: Create and interpret line plots with fractional data (1/2, 1/4, 1/8). Solve problems using fraction operations on plot data.
misconceptions: Students misplace data points between fractional tick marks
Students struggle computing with data from plots

diff.emergent: Read and create plots with halves
diff.proficient: Compute averages and differences from plot data

tools.name: Volume Builder
tools.description: Build rectangular prisms with unit cubes. Discover V = l × w × h through counting layers. Additive volume with composite shapes.
misconceptions: Students confuse area and volume
Students count faces instead of cubes
Students multiply only two dimensions

tools.name: Coordinate Graphing
tools.description: Plot and identify points in the first quadrant. Understand x/y axes, origin, and ordered pairs in real-world contexts.
misconceptions: Students reverse x and y coordinates
Students start counting from 1 instead of 0

diff.developing: Plot and identify in larger grids

tools.name: Shape Hierarchy
tools.description: Classify 2D figures in a hierarchy based on properties. Understand that attributes of a category apply to all subcategories.
misconceptions: Students think squares are not rectangles
Students classify by appearance rather than properties

tools.name: Ratio Table Builder
tools.description: Interactive ratio tables with tape diagrams and double number lines. Explore unit rates and proportional relationships visually.
misconceptions: Students use additive thinking instead of multiplicative
Students confuse ratios with fractions

diff.emergent: Complete ratio tables with visual support
diff.developing: Find unit rates and use them to solve problems
diff.proficient: Create and compare ratio tables from real-world scenarios

tools.name: Percent Bar & Grid
tools.description: Visual percent understanding with 100-grids and equivalence. Connect percents to fractions and decimals.
misconceptions: Students think percent means 100 items total
Students cannot connect percents to fractions and decimals

diff.emergent: Shade percent bars and read values
diff.developing: Match percents to fractions and decimals
diff.proficient: Solve real-world percent problems with flexible strategies

tools.name: Integer Number Line
tools.description: Explore negative numbers, opposites, and absolute value on a dynamic number line. Animated jumps and comparisons.
misconceptions: Students think -3 is greater than -1
Students confuse absolute value with the sign

diff.developing: Order integers and find absolute value
diff.proficient: Use integers to describe real-world contexts and reason about order

tools.name: Fraction Division Visual
tools.description: Measurement model for dividing fractions. Visual area models and tape diagrams with step-by-step interpretation.
misconceptions: Students invert both fractions (a÷b ÷ c÷d → a÷b × d÷c is wrong)
Students cannot interpret the quotient as a measurement

diff.emergent: Divide unit fractions by whole numbers visually
diff.developing: Use measurement model to divide fractions
diff.proficient: Represent and interpret fraction division in context

tools.name: GCF & LCM Finder
tools.description: Find GCF and LCM using factor trees and Venn diagrams. Prime factorization and practical applications.
misconceptions: Students confuse GCF with LCM
Students cannot connect prime factorization to GCF/LCM

diff.emergent: List factors and find GCF of two numbers
diff.developing: Use factor trees and Venn diagrams
diff.proficient: Apply GCF and LCM to solve real-world problems

tools.name: Expression Builder
tools.description: Algebra tiles for variable expressions and evaluation. Build and simplify expressions, substitute values.
misconceptions: Students see x as a label, not a quantity
Students add unlike terms (x + 3 = 3x)

diff.proficient: Simplify and interpret expressions in context

tools.name: Equation Solver
tools.description: Balance model for solving one-step equations. Apply operations to both sides, watch the balance respond.
misconceptions: Students apply operations to only one side
Students confuse inverse operations

diff.emergent: Solve one-step equations with positive integers
diff.developing: Record algebraic steps with balance model
diff.proficient: Solve one-step equations with all rational numbers

tools.name: Inequality Grapher
tools.description: Graph inequalities on a number line. Understand solutions as sets of numbers and interpret context.
misconceptions: Students reverse inequality direction when multiplying by negatives (Grade 7+)
Students do not understand solution sets

diff.emergent: Identify and graph solutions to simple inequalities
diff.developing: Match inequalities to their number line graphs
diff.proficient: Write and solve inequalities from real-world contexts

tools.name: Coordinate Grid Console
tools.description: Four-quadrant coordinate grid for plotting, distance, and polygon construction. Support for transformations.
misconceptions: Students confuse x and y coordinates
Students plot in wrong quadrants with negatives

diff.emergent: Plot points in Quadrant I only
diff.developing: Plot in all four quadrants and find distances
diff.proficient: Draw polygons and calculate areas on coordinate plane

tools.name: Area Decomposer
tools.description: Decompose polygons into triangles and rectangles to find area. Use formulas and count unit squares.
misconceptions: Students confuse area with perimeter
Students cannot see shapes in multiple ways

diff.emergent: Count unit squares to find areas
diff.proficient: Use decomposition to derive and apply area formulas

tools.name: Net Builder
tools.description: Unfold 3D shapes into nets and vice versa. Calculate surface area by finding area of net.
misconceptions: Students confuse surface area with volume
Students struggle to visualize 3D from 2D nets

diff.emergent: Identify nets of simple shapes like cubes
diff.proficient: Create nets and calculate surface area of polyhedra

tools.name: Data Display Builder
tools.description: Create and interpret dot plots, histograms, and box plots. Calculate mean, median, mode, range, and IQR.
misconceptions: Students confuse mean and median
Students do not see how data shape affects statistics

diff.emergent: Create dot plots and find median
diff.developing: Choose appropriate displays and calculate stats
diff.proficient: Compare distributions and explain shape, center, spread

tools.name: Statistical Questions
tools.description: Identify statistical questions, describe distributions, and understand variability. Compare data sets.
misconceptions: Students confuse statistical questions with non-statistical ones
Students do not understand variability

diff.proficient: Analyze and compare data sets, justify choices

tools.name: Proportion Solver
tools.description: Solve proportions using multiple strategies. Equivalent ratios, cross multiplication, and tape diagrams.
misconceptions: Students do not understand proportions as equal ratios
Students misuse cross multiplication

diff.emergent: Find missing values in equivalent ratios
diff.developing: Use multiple strategies to solve proportions
diff.proficient: Apply proportions to solve complex real-world problems

tools.name: Proportional Propulsion
tools.description: Identify proportional relationships using y = kx. Professional coordinate graphs, bar models, and adaptive 3-tier practice with step-by-step remediation.
misconceptions: Students confuse additive and multiplicative relationships
Students cannot identify k from a graph or table
Students think all linear relationships are proportional

diff.emergent: Whole-number constants with visual bar models
diff.advanced: Fractional constants of proportionality with full remediation

tools.name: Percent Applications
tools.description: Solve percent problems including tax, tip, discount, markup, and markup. Bar models and equations.
misconceptions: Students misapply percent to find discounted or taxed prices
Students do not understand percent as a rate

diff.developing: Apply percents to tips and tax problems
diff.proficient: Solve multi-step problems involving markups, markdowns, and compound percents

tools.name: Integer Operations
tools.description: Add, subtract, multiply, divide integers using chip/area models. Understand operations with signed numbers.
misconceptions: Students cannot connect operations on chips to symbolic notation
Students struggle with signs in multiplication/division

diff.emergent: Add and subtract integers with visual models
diff.proficient: Apply integer operations to solve real-world problems

tools.name: Rational Number Line
tools.description: Plot and order rational numbers (fractions, decimals, percents) on a zoomable number line.
misconceptions: Students struggle to order fractions and decimals
Students think -1.5 < -0.5 because 1.5 > 0.5

diff.developing: Locate and order fractions, decimals, and percents
diff.proficient: Find and use relationships between rational representations

tools.name: Expression Simplifier
tools.description: Combine like terms, use distributive property, and simplify expressions. Algebra tiles and symbolic.
misconceptions: Students add unlike terms
Students misapply distributive property

diff.emergent: Combine like terms with visual support
diff.proficient: Simplify complex expressions with multiple operations

tools.name: Two-Step Equations
tools.description: Solve two-step equations using balance model. Inverse operations and equivalent equations.
misconceptions: Students forget to apply operations to both sides
Students struggle with division of negative terms

diff.emergent: Solve two-step equations with positive coefficients
diff.proficient: Solve equations with variables on both sides

tools.name: Scale Drawing Lab
tools.description: Create and interpret scale drawings. Understand scale factor and apply to find actual and scale distances.
misconceptions: Students confuse scale with ratio
Students do not consistently apply scale factor

diff.emergent: Create scale drawings with given scale
diff.developing: Find scale factors and scale distances
diff.proficient: Use scale drawings to solve real-world geometry problems

tools.name: Cross-Section Viewer
tools.description: Visualize 3D shapes and their cross-sections. Rotate shapes and explore what happens when sliced.
misconceptions: Students struggle to visualize 3D shapes and their parts
Students cannot predict cross-sections

diff.emergent: Identify and describe cross-sections of simple shapes

tools.name: Circle Explorer
tools.description: Discover the relationship between circumference, diameter, and pi. Measure and calculate circle properties.
misconceptions: Students confuse diameter with radius or circumference
Students do not understand pi as a ratio

diff.developing: Calculate circumference and area using pi
diff.proficient: Apply circle properties to solve real-world problems

tools.name: Angle Relationships
tools.description: Explore complementary, supplementary, vertical, and adjacent angles. Understand angle sum in polygons.
misconceptions: Students confuse complementary and supplementary angles
Students do not understand angle relationships in context

diff.emergent: Identify angle relationships and find missing angles
diff.developing: Use angle relationships to solve problems
diff.proficient: Apply angle relationships to understand geometric figures

tools.name: Surface Area & Volume
tools.description: Calculate surface area and volume of prisms, pyramids, cylinders, and spheres. 3D visualization.
misconceptions: Students confuse surface area and volume
Students do not understand units (square vs. cubic)

diff.emergent: Calculate surface area and volume of prisms
diff.developing: Extend to pyramids, cylinders, and spheres
diff.proficient: Solve complex 3D problems and understand relationships

tools.name: Probability Simulator
tools.description: Simulate experiments with coins, dice, spinners, and compound events. Compare experimental vs theoretical probability.
misconceptions: Students think past outcomes affect future probability
Students confuse theoretical and experimental probability

diff.proficient: Design and analyze compound probability experiments

tools.name: Sampling & Inference
tools.description: Understand random sampling, draw inferences, and measure variability. Margin of error and predictions.
misconceptions: Students do not understand random sampling
Students cannot connect samples to populations

diff.proficient: Use samples to make predictions with confidence intervals

tools.name: Irrational Number Explorer
tools.description: Explore rational and irrational numbers. Approximate square roots and locate on zoomable number line.
misconceptions: Students think √2 ≈ 1.5 without justification
Students believe 22/7 = π exactly

diff.emergent: Estimate square roots between consecutive integers
diff.developing: Locate irrationals on number line with precision
diff.proficient: Explain why certain numbers are irrational

tools.name: Integer Exponents
tools.description: Understand powers, exponent rules, and scientific notation. Base, exponent, and order of operations.
misconceptions: Students think 2³ = 6
Students apply exponent rules incorrectly

diff.emergent: Evaluate simple powers and use basic exponent rules
diff.proficient: Justify exponent rules and solve power equations

tools.name: Scientific Notation
tools.description: Convert between standard and scientific notation. Operations with scientific notation and applications.
misconceptions: Students miscount powers of 10
Students misapply operations in scientific notation

diff.emergent: Convert between standard and scientific notation
diff.developing: Compare and order using scientific notation
diff.proficient: Perform operations with scientific notation in context

tools.name: Slope & Linear Equations
tools.description: Understand slope, y-intercept, and linear equations. Graph lines, interpret slope in context.
misconceptions: Students confuse slope with y-intercept
Students do not see slope as a rate of change

diff.emergent: Find slope and y-intercept from graphs
diff.developing: Write equations and graph linear relationships
diff.proficient: Interpret slope and y-intercept in real-world problems

tools.name: Systems of Equations
tools.description: Solve systems graphically and algebraically. Understand solutions and special cases (no solution, infinite).
misconceptions: Students mix methods and get confused
Students do not understand special cases

diff.proficient: Solve systems in context and interpret solutions

tools.name: Function Machine
tools.description: Input-output machines to understand functions. Create tables, graphs, and equations from machines.
misconceptions: Students think functions must be linear
Students confuse function notation with multiplication

diff.emergent: Use function machines and create tables
diff.proficient: Justify functions and write equations from context

tools.name: Linear vs Nonlinear
tools.description: Distinguish linear and nonlinear functions using tables, graphs, and equations. Understand rates of change.
misconceptions: Students think all curves are non-linear
Students do not connect constant rate to linearity

diff.emergent: Identify linear vs. nonlinear from graphs
diff.proficient: Distinguish and justify using multiple representations

tools.name: Slope-Intercept Form
tools.description: Understand y = mx + b. Explore parameters m and b with sliders and real-world contexts.
misconceptions: Students think b is the x-intercept
Students confuse slope direction

diff.emergent: Identify m and b from graphs and equations
diff.proficient: Model real-world situations with linear equations

tools.name: Transformation Lab
tools.description: Translate, rotate, reflect, dilate on coordinate plane. Discover congruence and similarity through transformations.
misconceptions: Students confuse rotation direction
Students apply translation as multiplication

tools.name: Congruence & Similarity
tools.description: Understand congruence and similarity through transformations. AA, SSS, SAS criteria and applications.
misconceptions: Students confuse congruence with similarity
Students assume visual similarity means congruence

diff.proficient: Apply to solve problems and prove relationships

tools.name: Pythagorean Theorem Explorer
tools.description: Discover and apply the Pythagorean theorem. Area models, distance formula, and real-world applications.
misconceptions: Students do not understand why the theorem works
Students misidentify the hypotenuse

diff.emergent: Verify Pythagorean theorem with area models
diff.developing: Apply theorem to find missing sides
diff.proficient: Use Pythagorean theorem in complex problems

tools.name: Volume: Cones, Cylinders, Spheres
tools.description: Calculate volumes of cylinders, cones, and spheres. Understand formulas through 3D visualization.
misconceptions: Students confuse volume and surface area
Students do not understand relationships between formulas

diff.proficient: Apply volume formulas to solve problems

tools.name: Scatter Plots & Trends
tools.description: Create scatter plots, identify trends, and fit lines of best fit. Understand correlation and causation.
misconceptions: Students confuse correlation with causation
Students cannot interpret slopes in context

diff.emergent: Create scatter plots and identify trends
diff.proficient: Analyze bivariate data and make predictions

tools.name: Solving Linear Equations
tools.description: Solve linear equations with all rational coefficients. Multiple methods and justifications.
misconceptions: Students apply operations inconsistently
Students struggle with negative coefficients

diff.proficient: Solve equations with variables on both sides

tools.name: Linear Inequalities
tools.description: Solve linear inequalities and graph on number line. Understand solution sets and real-world constraints.
misconceptions: Students reverse inequality when multiplying by negatives
Students do not understand solution sets

diff.proficient: Apply inequalities to solve real-world problems

tools.name: Graphing Linear Functions
tools.description: Graph linear functions using various forms. Identify key "features": intercepts, slope, domain, range.
misconceptions: Students confuse slope with y-intercept
Students do not understand domain and range in context

diff.proficient: Graph functions in various forms and identify key features

tools.name: Systems by Graphing
tools.description: Solve systems of linear equations by graphing. Interpret solutions as intersection points.
misconceptions: Students misread intersection points
Students do not check solutions

diff.emergent: Solve systems by graphing with integer solutions
diff.proficient: Solve and interpret in real-world contexts

tools.name: Quadratic Explorer
tools.description: Explore quadratic functions and their transformations. Vertex, axis of symmetry, and real-world models.
misconceptions: Students do not see parabolas in vertex form
Students confuse direction and width

tools.name: Factoring Lab
tools.description: Factor quadratics using area models, GCF, trinomials. Connect to graphing and solving.
misconceptions: Students do not connect factoring to zeros of functions
Students factor incompletely

diff.emergent: Factor using GCF and simple trinomials
diff.proficient: Use factoring to solve and analyze functions

tools.name: Quadratic Formula
tools.description: Derive and apply the quadratic formula. Understand discriminant and complex solutions.
misconceptions: Students misapply the formula
Students do not understand the discriminant

diff.emergent: Apply quadratic formula to solve equations
diff.proficient: Solve complex quadratics and interpret in context

tools.name: Exponent Rules & Radicals
tools.description: Understand exponent rules, rational exponents, and radical expressions. Simplify and solve.
misconceptions: Students misapply exponent rules
Students do not understand rational exponents as radicals

diff.emergent: Apply basic exponent and radical rules
diff.proficient: Solve radical equations and justify rules

tools.name: Sequence Explorer
tools.description: Explore arithmetic and geometric sequences. Find patterns, write formulas, and model exponential growth.
misconceptions: Students confuse arithmetic and geometric
Students do not connect sequences to functions

diff.proficient: Model and solve problems with sequences

tools.name: One-Variable Statistics
tools.description: Analyze "distributions": mean, median, mode, standard deviation, quartiles, box plots, histograms.
misconceptions: Students do not understand measures of variation
Students misinterpret outliers

diff.emergent: Calculate measures of center and spread

tools.name: Logic & Proofs
tools.description: Understand logical reasoning, conditional statements, and structure of geometric proofs.
misconceptions: Students confuse converse with conditional
Students cannot connect logic to geometric proof

tools.name: Triangle Congruence
tools.description: Explore triangle congruence "criteria": SSS, SAS, ASA, AAS. Construct and prove congruence.
misconceptions: Students assume visual congruence without justification
Students confuse SSA with SAS

tools.name: Similarity & Dilations
tools.description: Understand similarity through dilations and transformations. AA, SSS, SAS for similarity.
misconceptions: Students confuse congruence with similarity
Students do not scale all sides consistently

diff.emergent: Identify similar figures using scale factor
diff.developing: Use similarity to find missing sides
diff.proficient: Prove similarity and solve complex problems

tools.name: Right Triangle Trig
tools.description: Understand and apply trigonometric "ratios": sine, cosine, tangent. Solve right triangles.
misconceptions: Students confuse which ratio to use
Students do not understand ratios as side relationships

diff.emergent: Find trig ratios from right triangles
diff.developing: Use trig to find missing sides

tools.name: Circle Theorems
tools.description: Explore theorems about chords, secants, tangents, and inscribed angles. Relationships and proofs.
misconceptions: Students do not see relationships between angles and arcs
Students confuse different circle theorems

diff.emergent: Identify relationships between angles and arcs
diff.developing: Use theorems to find missing measures
diff.proficient: Prove circle theorems and solve complex problems

tools.name: Coordinate Geometry Proofs
tools.description: Use coordinates to prove geometric relationships. Distance, midpoint, slope, and equations of circles.
misconceptions: Students do not see coordinate geometry as proof tool
Students misapply distance or midpoint formulas

diff.proficient: Prove theorems and solve problems with coordinate geometry

tools.name: Area & Volume Modeling
tools.description: Understand how area and volume formulas are derived. Cavalieri's principle and 3D relationships.
misconceptions: Students apply formulas without understanding
Students confuse units and relationships

diff.developing: Apply formulas to find areas and volumes

tools.description: Find probabilities using geometric measurement. Area ratios and practical applications.
misconceptions: Students do not see geometry in probability
Students misapply area and ratio

tools.name: Polynomial Operations
tools.description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide polynomials. Understand closure and properties.
misconceptions: Students do not combine like terms
Students misapply distributive property

tools.name: Rational Expressions
tools.description: Simplify, add, subtract, multiply, divide rational expressions. Understand restrictions.
misconceptions: Students cancel incorrectly
Students do not identify domain restrictions

diff.developing: Add and subtract with unlike denominators

tools.name: Radical Functions
tools.description: Graph and analyze radical functions. Domain restrictions, transformations, and applications.
misconceptions: Students do not understand domain restrictions
Students misapply transformations

diff.proficient: Analyze and model with radical functions

tools.name: Exponential & Log Functions
tools.description: Understand exponential and logarithmic functions as inverses. Solve equations, model real-world situations.
misconceptions: Students do not see log as inverse of exponential
Students misapply log properties

diff.emergent: Graph and evaluate exponential and log functions
diff.developing: Solve equations with exponentials and logs
diff.proficient: Model and analyze real-world exponential situations

tools.name: Trigonometric Functions
tools.description: Understand periodic functions. Graph sine, cosine, tangent with transformations and real-world models.
misconceptions: Students do not understand periodicity
Students confuse radian and degree measures

diff.emergent: Graph trig functions and identify key features

tools.name: Complex Numbers
tools.description: Understand complex numbers, operations, and properties. Connect to quadratics and solving.
misconceptions: Students do not understand i as a number
Students misapply operations with complex numbers

tools.name: Conic Sections
tools.description: Explore parabolas, circles, ellipses, hyperbolas. Equations, transformations, and applications.
misconceptions: Students do not connect algebra to geometry
Students do not see conic section relationships

diff.emergent: Graph conic sections and identify form
diff.proficient: Analyze conics and solve complex problems

diff.proficient: Use regression to model and analyze real-world data

tools.description: Explore limits intuitively through graphs and tables. Left/right limits and continuity.
misconceptions: Students think limit equals function value
Students do not understand one-sided limits

tools.name: Unit Circle Mastery
tools.description: Master the unit circle with exact values, radians, and trig identities.
misconceptions: Students memorize without understanding geometry
Students confuse special angles

diff.proficient: Use unit circle to verify identities

tools.name: Polar Coordinates
tools.description: Convert between Cartesian and polar coordinates. Graph polar equations and understand curves.
misconceptions: Students do not visualize polar curves
Students confuse angle and radius

tools.description: Explore parametric curves. Convert between parametric and Cartesian, understand motion.
misconceptions: Students do not see parameter as time or distance
Students struggle to eliminate parameter

diff.emergent: Plot parametric curves and identify features
diff.proficient: Model motion and analyze parametric curves

tools.name: Vectors Introduction
tools.description: Understand vectors, operations, magnitude, direction. Applications in motion and forces.
misconceptions: Students confuse vectors with points
Students misapply operations

tools.name: Series & Sequences
tools.description: Explore sequences and series. Arithmetic, geometric, and convergence concepts.
misconceptions: Students do not understand convergence
Students confuse series and sequences

diff.proficient: Analyze and apply series in context

tools.name: Coin & Bill Identification
tools.description: Identify coins and bills, learn their values, and practice counting money.

tools.name: Fair Games Explorer
tools.description: Explore probability through coins, spinners, and dice. Determine if games are fair or unfair.

tools.name: Money Decisions
tools.description: Make spending and saving decisions with virtual coins. Compare prices and count change.

tools.name: Probability Predictions
tools.description: Pull marbles from bags, track frequencies, and make predictions about outcomes.

tools.name: Algorithm Sorter
tools.description: Arrange step-by-step procedure cards in correct order. Build algorithmic thinking.

tools.name: Growth Pattern Predictor
tools.description: Build growing patterns with tiles. Find the rule and predict next terms.

tools.name: Data Collection Methods
tools.description: Design surveys, collect virtual responses, and build graphs. Learn good data collection.

tools.name: Balance Puzzle Equations
tools.description: Use a virtual balance scale to find unknown values. Pre-algebra reasoning.

tools.name: Needs vs Wants
tools.description: Sort items into needs and wants categories. Prioritize spending decisions.

tools.name: Multiplicative Relationships
tools.description: Explore times-as-many relationships with visual ratio tables and multiplicative comparisons.

tools.name: School Layout Designer
tools.description: Design rooms on a grid, calculate area and perimeter with architectural constraints.

tools.name: Transformation Explorer
tools.description: Move shapes on a grid using slides, flips, and turns. Identify transformations.

tools.name: Variability Explorer
tools.description: Create data sets and see range/spread update live. Identify outliers and compare variability.

tools.name: Budget Basics
tools.description: Create simple budgets with income and expenses. Balance budgets and handle surprises.

tools.name: Recipe Multiplier
tools.description: Scale recipes up and down using fraction multiplication. Real-world proportional reasoning.

tools.name: Temperature Measurement
tools.description: Interactive thermometer for reading temperatures in Fahrenheit and Celsius.

tools.name: Efficiency Explorer
tools.description: Solve the same problem multiple ways. Compare strategies for efficiency.

tools.name: Logic Puzzle Solver
tools.description: Interactive logic grid puzzles using if-then reasoning. Eliminate possibilities with clues.

tools.name: Variable Notation Mastery
tools.description: Build algebraic expressions by dragging variables. Match word phrases to expressions.

tools.name: Expression Evaluation Explorer
tools.description: Substitute values into expressions and check if values satisfy equations.

tools.name: Dependent & Independent Variables
tools.description: Interactive cause/effect scenarios. Identify independent and dependent variables.

tools.name: Long Division with Decimals
tools.description: Step-by-step visual long division with decimal placement and scaffolding.

tools.name: Negative Numbers Deep Dive
tools.description: Interactive number line with positive/negative. Compare, order, and find absolute values.

tools.name: Volume & Surface Area Relationships
tools.description: 3D shape builder with visual volume and surface area calculations.

tools.name: Statistical Inquiry Tool
tools.description: Walk through the full statistical "process": Question, Data, Analysis, Conclusion.

tools.name: Distribution Comparison
tools.description: Build two dot plots side by side. Compare shape, center, and spread.

tools.name: Multi-Representation Viewer
tools.description: See relationships as table, graph, equation, and words simultaneously.

tools.name: Concrete Math Lab
tools.description: Virtual "manipulatives": algebra tiles, fraction bars, integer chips. Model equations visually.

tools.name: Interest Calculator
tools.description: Visual compound interest growth. Calculate simple and compound interest.

tools.name: Credit & Debt Basics
tools.description: Simulate credit card purchases. Calculate minimum payments, total interest, and payoff time.

tools.name: Correlation Explorer
tools.description: Plot scatter plots and see correlation lines update. Identify correlation types.

tools.name: Polynomial Introduction
tools.description: Visual polynomial builder with algebra tiles. Add/subtract polynomials.

tools.name: Basic Transformations
tools.description: Interactive grid for translations, rotations, and reflections.

tools.name: Proof Builder
tools.description: Drag-and-drop proof blocks for two-column proofs. Arrange statements and reasons.

tools.name: Addition Quest
tools.description: Number line adventure game with animated hop scaffolding. Students see addition as movement on a number line with remedial visual proof on errors.
misconceptions: Students count the starting number as a hop
Students lose track of hops when crossing decade boundaries
Students confuse the landing position with number of hops

tools.name: Division Dash
tools.description: Area model game with animated group-by-group partitioning. Students visualize division as equal grouping with step-through remedial scaffolding.
misconceptions: Students confuse divisor with quotient
Students ignore remainders
Students cannot connect repeated subtraction to division

tools.name: Socratic: Fraction Multiply & Divide
tools.description: Guided Socratic questioning through fraction multiplication and division with visual models, branching paths, and scaffolded hints.
misconceptions: Students multiply both numerators and denominators when dividing
Students think dividing always makes smaller

diff.emergent: Guided with sentence frames and visuals

tools.name: Socratic: Ratios & Rates
tools.description: Socratic exploration of ratios, unit rates, and proportional reasoning through lemonade recipe scaling.
misconceptions: Students add instead of multiply when scaling ratios
Students confuse ratio order (a:b vs b:a)

diff.emergent: Guided with sentence frames and ratio tables

tools.name: Socratic: Equivalence & Representations
tools.description: Socratic exploration comparing fractions, decimals, and percents through shopping discounts.
misconceptions: Students compare fractions by numerator only
Students think 1/3 = 0.3 exactly

tools.name: Socratic: Percent
tools.description: Socratic exploration of percent as 'per hundred' with real-world applications in shopping and scoring.
misconceptions: Students confuse finding percent OF vs finding THE percent
Students forget to convert percent to decimal before multiplying

diff.emergent: Visual percent bars with guided steps

tools.name: Socratic: Proportional Relationships
tools.description: Discover proportional relationships through road trip data tables, graphs, and equations.
misconceptions: Students think all linear relationships are proportional
Students confuse additive and multiplicative relationships

diff.emergent: Ratio tables with guided pattern finding

tools.name: Socratic: Solving Equations
tools.description: Socratic exploration of equation solving as 'undoing' operations through mystery number puzzles.
misconceptions: Students perform operations on one side only
Students undo operations in the wrong order

diff.emergent: Mystery number puzzles with visual support
diff.advanced: Writes and solves equations from word problems

tools.name: Socratic: Pythagorean Theorem
tools.description: Discover the Pythagorean theorem through area reasoning and tilted squares.
misconceptions: Students apply theorem to non-right triangles
Students confuse legs with hypotenuse

diff.proficient: Applies theorem to find missing sides

tools.name: Socratic: Locate & Compare Numbers
tools.description: Navigate fractions, decimals, and percents on the number line through a hiking trail story.
misconceptions: Students think larger denominator means larger fraction
Students struggle placing negatives on number line

tools.name: Socratic: Factors, GCF & LCM
tools.description: Discover factors, primes, GCF, and LCM through party planning and rectangle arrangements.
misconceptions: Students confuse GCF and LCM
Students forget 1 is a factor of every number

diff.emergent: Factor pairs with area model visuals

tools.name: Socratic: Applying Rational Numbers
tools.description: Operations with positive and negative numbers through bank accounts and temperature changes.
misconceptions: Students think subtracting a negative is the same as subtracting a positive
Students apply integer rules incorrectly to fractions

tools.name: Socratic: Mean, Median & Range
tools.description: Discover measures of center through basketball scoring statistics.
misconceptions: Students forget to order data before finding median
Students think mean and median are always equal

tools.name: Socratic: Circumference & Area of Circles
tools.description: Discover pi, circumference, and area through circular garden design.
misconceptions: Students confuse radius and diameter
Students think doubling radius doubles area

diff.advanced: Explores area scaling with radius changes

tools.name: Socratic: Representing & Comparing Rationals
tools.description: Master converting and comparing rational numbers in all forms through recipe scaling.
misconceptions: Students compare negatives by magnitude alone
Students think -3/4 > 0.5 because 3/4 > 0.5

tools.name: Socratic: Equations, Inequalities & Systems
tools.description: From single equations to systems — discover intersection points through phone plan comparisons.
misconceptions: Students think systems always have one solution
Students flip inequality when not dividing by negative

tools.name: Socratic: Similarity & Scaling
tools.description: Discover similarity through architecture — scale models, proportional sides, and area scaling.
misconceptions: Students think scale factor applies to area directly
Students confuse congruent with similar

diff.proficient: Finds missing sides using scale factor

tools.name: Socratic: Rational, Irrational & Real Numbers
tools.description: Discover irrational numbers through the diagonal of a square and classify the real number system.
misconceptions: Students think all decimals are rational
Students think pi = 3.14 exactly

diff.advanced: Explains why sqrt of non-perfect squares is irrational

tools.name: Prime or Composite Explorer
tools.description: 3D interactive 12×12 multiplication grid where students discover primes and composites through Socratic guided questioning. Features Learn/Check modes, flat factor tiles, glowing sphere arrays, CRA progression, wrong-answer remediation, and three discovery sequences (Even Sweep, Sieve, Factor Density).
misconceptions: Students think 1 is prime
Students confuse factors and multiples
Students think all even numbers are composite (forgetting 2)
Students miss that perfect squares have a repeated factor pair

diff.emergent: Guided Learn mode with scaffolded even/odd and factor checks
diff.proficient: Check mode with self-assessment of prime vs composite
diff.advanced: Discovery sequences: Sieve, Factor Density visualization

tools.name: Divisibility Rules Explorer
tools.description: 3D interactive app teaching divisibility rules (2–12) on a multiplication chart. Features animated digit sum visualizer, intersection mode with Venn diagrams showing GCD/LCM, rule-specific Learn flows with Socratic questioning, and practice mode with score tracking.
misconceptions: Students confuse divisibility rules for 3 and 9
Students think divisibility by 6 requires a separate rule rather than combining 2 and 3
Students forget the digit sum rule applies recursively

diff.emergent: Explore mode with guided highlighting of multiples
diff.proficient: Learn mode with Socratic divisibility rule discovery
diff.advanced: Intersection mode with GCD/LCM and practice with numbers to 999
